channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Mspolicy] If You Want Parent Power, Only HB2 Will Do
item.description:
Over the past few years, Mississippi lawmakers have passed some critical conservative reforms. Last year, Mississippi became the first state in America to legislate to eliminate the income tax in 40 years.  In 2022, we implemented flat tax reform.  A few years before that, we passed important labor market reforms.  In 2024, we reformed school funding […]

The post If You Want Parent Power, Only HB2 Will Do appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Rifreedom] MEDIA RELEASE: Center Launches Petition Calling for Affordable Energy Policies
item.description:
The Rhode Island Center for Freedom & Prosperity today announced that it has launched an online petition drive calling for policymakers to support a fundamental pivot away from our state's current energy strategy, which will lead to staggeringly high electricity prices and potentially dangerous blackouts.

The post MEDIA RELEASE: Center Launches Petition Calling for Affordable Energy Policies appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] The Denial of Distinctions
item.description:
The Denial of Distinctions By Abe Greenwald Yesterday, I wrote about the return of American disorder in the form of mobs that obstruct ICE raids. Part of what makes anti-ICE/open-border activism so strange is that it attacks the most fundamental aspect of what it means to be a country: its physical boundaries. When leftists repeat, […]

The post The Denial of Distinctions appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Crime is down, and it should end ‘root cause’ excuse-making for good
item.description: The post Crime is down, and it should end ‘root cause’ excuse-making for good appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Beacontn] Introducing TennSight, Tennessee’s only hub for voter insights
item.description: We hope that TennSight will become the go-to source for you to learn what matters most to your fellow Tennesseans, why it matters, and what can be done about it.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Poll: January 2026 Results
item.description: Today, we released the latest installment of the Beacon Poll, a statewide survey of 1,200 Tennessee voters featuring questions about voters’ views on property taxes, the federal school choice tax credit included in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, and political approval ratings at the state and federal level, and more. Tennesseans continue to prioritize key...

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] Op-Ed: Hospitals Collect Tax Breaks. West Virginians Collect Medical Debt.
item.description:
To many West Virginians, healthcare continues to be a burden more than a lifeline as roughly 180,000 residents, or 13.3% of adults, now facing some sort of  medical debt. This is well above the national average of 8.6% and, even worse, nearly one in four of these residents in rural Appalachia have medical debt in […]

The post Op-Ed: Hospitals Collect Tax Breaks. West Virginians Collect Medical Debt. appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Tim Pawlenty: The Crisis Will Subside in Minnesota, but Trust Will Need to be Rebuilt
item.description:
We’ll all be part of that task. Here’s where efforts could be focused. By Tim Pawlenty For the Star Tribune It’s been said that trust is the glue that holds relationships together. That may well explain why recent events in Minnesota have stretched our relationship with government to the breaking point. Fraudsters stole a ton […]

The post Tim Pawlenty: The Crisis Will Subside in Minnesota, but Trust Will Need to be Rebuilt appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] The Numbers Don’t Lie: The CHOOSE Act is Empowering Alabama’s Families
item.description:
For decades, the Alabama Policy Institute has championed a simple, powerful idea: every child in Alabama deserves a high-quality education, regardless of their zip code or their size of their parents’ bank account. With the passage and implementation of the CHOOSE Act, that vision is finally becoming a reality.

The post The Numbers Don’t Lie: The CHOOSE Act is Empowering Alabama’s Families appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] 312 Out of More than 3 Million Votes
item.description:
Federal prosecutors are closing in on prosecuting more than 100 Minnesotans for rampant fraud in several social service programs designed to serve children and low-income residents. By the time the US Attorney’s office is done investigating, charging, convicting, and sentencing these felons, this unchecked fraud will significantly increase Minnesota’s prison population. But there’s one other […]

The post 312 Out of More than 3 Million Votes appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Rifreedom] TESTIMONY: Senate Commission to Study the Successful Implementation of the Act on Climate
item.description:
In this powerful testimony, Mike Stenhouse, CEO of the RI Center for Freedom & Prosperity, explains why energy policy—not taxes, not housing, not government services—is the single most important determinant of Rhode Island’s future prosperity or poverty. Whether one believes in climate change or not, the economic consequences of the Act on Climate are unavoidable.

The post TESTIMONY: Senate Commission to Study the Successful Implementation of the Act on Climate appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Governor Mills’ Supplemental Budget Abandons Fiscal Discipline, Sets Maine Up for Higher Costs
item.description:
Governor Janet Mills’ FY 2026 supplemental budget marks a clear break from the fiscal restraint her administration often claimed to practice. Rather than using Maine’s strong revenue growth to stabilize taxpayers after years of tax increases and inflation, the proposal dramatically expands spending, broadens emergency powers, and doubles down on distortionary tax policy choices that […]

The post Governor Mills’ Supplemental Budget Abandons Fiscal Discipline, Sets Maine Up for Higher Costs appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Affordability tops the list of state priorities for 2026
item.description: The post Affordability tops the list of state priorities for 2026 appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Ayotte: Maximize economic freedom, and New Hampshire will thrive
item.description:
Gov. Kelly Ayotte’s 2026 State of the State address can be summed up in seven words: New Hampshire wins by competing on freedom. Leading with a list of eight No. 1 national rankings for the state—including economic freedom, economic opportunity and taxpayer return on investment—Ayotte noted that, overall, “U.S. News ranks us the #2 state […]

The post Ayotte: Maximize economic freedom, and New Hampshire will thrive appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Senate unanimously passes App Store Accountability Act
item.description:
The App Store Accountability Act passed the Alabama Senate with a unanimous vote on Feb. 5. The bill is focused on online safety for children and teens by requiring parental approval for kids to download apps from the App Store.

The post Alabama Senate unanimously passes App Store Accountability Act appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Senate passes App Store age verification requirement
item.description:
Lawmakers spoke before the Senate in favor of shielding minors from inappropriate content, requiring parental permission for account creation.

The post Alabama Senate passes App Store age verification requirement appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Strengthening families for generational change: GCO welcomes Travis Thorne to the team
item.description: The post Strengthening families for generational change: GCO welcomes Travis Thorne to the team appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Florida Politics: Florida’s A+ Plan To Help ‘Plan B’ Families
item.description:
It was a curious finding that seemed to make no sense at all. Last year, more than 41,000...

The post Florida Politics: Florida’s A+ Plan To Help ‘Plan B’ Families appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Domestic Outmigration is Hollowing Out Massachusetts’ Workforce and Economy
item.description:
From April 2020 through July 2025, Massachusetts experienced a net loss of approximately 182,000 residents to domestic outmigration — the equivalent of losing one-and-a-half Cambridges. While a recent surge in international immigration temporarily boosted labor force numbers, new research from Pioneer Institute shows that underlying demographic, workforce, and economic challenges continue to threaten the Commonwealth’s long-term competitiveness. 

The post Domestic Outmigration is Hollowing Out Massachusetts’ Workforce and Economy  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Bipartisan electoral politics stand in the way of property tax relief
item.description: Kansas homeowners aren’t just facing higher property taxes — they’re facing unaffordable assessed valuation increases that make it impossible to budget for life essentials or stay in their homes. Property tax is the #1 affordability issue facing many Kansans, but nothing may be done about it – again – because electoral politics is the #1 […]

item.title: [Mainepolicy] An Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature: Stop Advancing a Bill You Know is Unconstitutional
item.description:
Last June, Maine Policy Institute urged Governor Janet Mills to veto LD 1666, a bill expanding ranked-choice voting (RCV) to elections for governor, state senator, and state representative. That request was grounded in clear constitutional text, the Maine Supreme Judicial Court’s 2017 advisory opinion, and Governor Mills’ own legal analysis as Attorney General. Since then, […]

The post An Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature: Stop Advancing a Bill You Know is Unconstitutional appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Texas Parents Break Records for School Choice Applications
item.description:
By Kathryn Hickok At last count, more than one and a half million...

The post Texas Parents Break Records for School Choice Applications appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Metro’s Plan for Making Housing More Expensive
item.description:
By Randal O’Toole High housing prices in the Portland area led to a legislative...

The post Metro’s Plan for Making Housing More Expensive appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York’s Electricity Prices Among the Highest in the Country
item.description:
Albany, NY — New York households continue to pay some of the highest electricity prices in the nation, according to data from....

The post New York’s Electricity Prices Among the Highest in the Country appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Newsmax: CMS Should Be Embracing Fla.’s Medicaid Success Story
item.description:
A decade ago, as speaker of the Florida House of Representatives, I led the fight against Medicaid expansion under Obamacare....

The post Newsmax: CMS Should Be Embracing Fla.’s Medicaid Success Story appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Portland’s “Affordable” Housing Averages $800 per Square Foot
item.description:
By Randal O’Toole Note: This post is a sequel to Metro’s Plan for...

The post Portland’s “Affordable” Housing Averages $800 per Square Foot appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation Files Unfair Labor Practice Complaint Against Ohio Union after Bus Drivers Blocked from Stopping Dues Deductions
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today announced it has filed an unfair labor practice complaint against the Ohio Association of Public School Employees (OAPSE) after the union refused to stop collecting dues from seven Symmes Valley school bus drivers who had properly resigned their membership.

The post Freedom Foundation Files Unfair Labor Practice Complaint Against Ohio Union after Bus Drivers Blocked from Stopping Dues Deductions appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Statement of Jim Stergios, Executive Director of Pioneer Institute on Governor Healey’s Opposition to Lowering State Income Tax
item.description:
On Friday, Governor Healey opposed a proposed cut in the personal income tax that would put an average of $1,300 back into people’s pockets. Her claim that it would devastate the budget and gut education is a wild distortion—and it ignores more...

The post Statement of Jim Stergios, Executive Director of Pioneer Institute on Governor Healey’s Opposition to Lowering State Income Tax appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Center Launches K-12 Student Video Contest to Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday #America250RI
item.description:
In recognition of President's Day and George Washington's upcoming birthday, the RI Center for Freedom & Prosperity today launched a video contest encouraging K-12 students to create a video about why they are proud to celebrate America's semiquincentennial, commemorating the 250th Anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

The post Center Launches K-12 Student Video Contest to Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday #America250RI appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation Files Unfair Labor Practice Complaint Against AFSCME After Union Refuses to Stop Dues Deductions for Toledo School Secretary
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today announced it has filed an unfair labor practice complaint with the Ohio State Employment Relations Board (SERB) against AFSCME Ohio Council 8 after the union refused to stop collecting dues from Sandra Nelson, a Toledo Public Schools secretary who properly resigned her membership — twice.

The post Freedom Foundation Files Unfair Labor Practice Complaint Against AFSCME After Union Refuses to Stop Dues Deductions for Toledo School Secretary appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Economic Updates Suggest Kansas Can Lead
item.description: If you only look at Kansas’s unemployment rate, you might think everything is fine. That’s the political trap: point to a low number, declare victory, and move on. But the latest state labor and economic output data show a more complicated truth. Kansas has real strengths, but it’s also leaving growth on the table because […]

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] A Roadmap for States to Build High-Quality Online Education
item.description:
BOSTON – State policy is pivotal to the development and availability of online and virtual learning options, and Pioneer Institute’s new Virtual Schools Toolkit provides tactical guidance for developing such policies in the current educational landscape.   “Virtual learning began by serving students...

The post A Roadmap for States to Build High-Quality Online Education appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Florida Politics: Anastasios Kamoutsas Curbs Double Counting in K-12 Scholarships
item.description:
February 16, 2026 Administrative fix delivers zero net cost to state Anastasios Kamoutsas has been in his new job...

The post Florida Politics: Anastasios Kamoutsas Curbs Double Counting in K-12 Scholarships appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Legislators look at local zoning rules blocking education, child care & home businesses
item.description:
Local land use regulations do more than severely restrict the size, shape, design and placement of homes, creating scarcity and driving up prices. They also heavily regulate the activities that occur inside those homes. These regulations derive from legitimate local police powers. Local governments exist primarily for the purpose of keeping the peace, and Americans […]

The post Legislators look at local zoning rules blocking education, child care & home businesses appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture
item.description:
West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture Debates over hospital policy in West Virginia usually follow a familiar pattern. Hospital systems warn that their finances are weak. Industry groups say they have too many patients with government insurance. Certificate of Need (CON) rules are defended as needed to keep […]

The post West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] We Can’t Afford to Trim Your Trees, but We Can Hire Lobbyists
item.description: We’ve long called for reform to the practice of taxpayer-funded lobbying. But this NES response is probably the most glaring example of the need for reform.

item.title: [Empowerms] Aaron Rice: Defending the Constitution in Uniform and in Court
item.description:
Aaron Rice: Defending the Constitution in Uniform and in Court What does it look like to defend the Constitution with your life, and then keep defending it with your career? In this episode, Grant sits down with Aaron Rice, a Mississippi Marine and Purple Heart recipient who was wounded in Iraq and later became a…

The post Aaron Rice: Defending the Constitution in Uniform and in Court first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Mamdani Gets an Important Tax Fact Wrong
item.description:
At a hearing in Albany last week, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani lobbied state lawmakers to help him balance....

The post Mamdani Gets an Important Tax Fact Wrong appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Reducing Red Tape: Advancing Economic Liberty and Accountability In Nebraska
item.description:
The growing volume and complexity of government regulation at the state and local levels threaten economic freedom, entrepreneurial innovation, and democratic accountability. State regulations, often issued by unelected officials in executive agencies, accumulate over time, creating significant “hidden taxes” on small businesses and families within the state. This policy brief outlines a set of proven,...

The post Reducing Red Tape: Advancing Economic Liberty and Accountability In Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation Challenges Oregon’s Unconstitutional Speech Law in Federal Court
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today presented oral arguments in federal court seeking a preliminary injunction against Oregon HB 3789, a law that threatens the organization with millions in penalties for informing public employees about their constitutional rights.

The post Freedom Foundation Challenges Oregon’s Unconstitutional Speech Law in Federal Court   appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] 2025 data is in: Four straight years of unaffordable assessed valuation hikes. Where’s the relief?
item.description: Assessed valuation changes for 2025, released this morning, reiterate the need for a constitutional amendment to limit the increases. The average increase for existing residential property (not counting new construction) of 5.9% is more than double the inflation rate, and that’s not the worst of the story. Homeowners in a dozen counties had double-digit increases […]

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: History Matters, Ms. Ocasio-Cortez IT IS EASY to make fun of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (AOC) and her flubs. It is clear she doesn’t  understand the dynamics that created America or her own heritage. But she is not alone. It would be surprising if there are 20 Indiana legislators who would fare better. Surveys of how little this generation understands of Western Civilization are so discouraging that Read the full article...

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law
item.description:
Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law IRG has several outstanding requests that have been pending for months  Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) has waited over eight months for records on DOJ immigration enforcement and the Evers Administration’s Office […]

The post Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Mspolicy] New Certificate of Need Law – A Small Free Market Win
item.description:
A small win for freedom in Mississippi!  Governor Tate Reeves recently signed HB3, a law that reforms the state’s Certificate of Need (CON) rules.  CON is basically a government approval process healthcare providers (like hospitals) must go through before they can add certain services, build new facilities, buy expensive equipment, or make big expansions. HB3 […]

The post New Certificate of Need Law – A Small Free Market Win appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] What Will California Gas Prices Do in 2026?
item.description:
About the time it became inevitable that California was going to lose two major refineries, in May of last year, an alarming study was released by Michael Mische, an economist and business professor at USC. In his analysis, “Ensuring California’s Gasoline Security for the 21st Century,” Mische made a prediction that was widely quoted: “Based on current...

The post What Will California Gas Prices Do in 2026? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Will Law] Legislature Grants Initial Approval for WILL-Backed Constitutional Amendment to Protect Legislative Oversight
item.description:
The Wisconsin State Senate approved Assembly Joint Resolution 133 (AJR 133) on first consideration, a WILL-supported proposed amendment to the Wisconsin Constitution designed to restore legislative oversight of administrative rulemaking.

The post Legislature Grants Initial Approval for WILL-Backed Constitutional Amendment to Protect Legislative Oversight appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Credit Card Price Controls Aren’t a Shortcut to Affordability
item.description:
When costs rise and families feel squeezed, Washington’s reflex is always the same: cap prices, mandate terms, declare victory. It’s..

The post Credit Card Price Controls Aren’t a Shortcut to Affordability appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Virginiainstitute] The Case Against SB 269
item.description:
In Virginia's 2026 legislative session, Senate Bill 269 (patroned by Senator Favola) is a flashpoint for debate regarding the role of technology in mental healthcare. While proponents argue it creates necessary guardrails, it is a solution in search of a problem that could stifle innovation and limit patient access to care.

The post The Case Against SB 269 first appeared on Virginia Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Another Step for Improving Missouri’s Licensing Regime
item.description:
In occupational licensing, Missouri has been ahead of the curve. In 2020, the state established a universal licensing reciprocity regime, allowing licensed professionals from other states to have Missouri requirements […]

The post Another Step for Improving Missouri’s Licensing Regime appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Missouri Doesn’t Have To Be Kansas
item.description:
A version of the following commentary appeared in the St. Louis Post-Dispatch. In his January 30 op-ed for the Post-Dispatch, Kansas political scientist Michael Smith called Governor Mike Kehoe’s proposal […]

The post Missouri Doesn’t Have To Be Kansas appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] California High-Speed Rail at a Crossroads
item.description:
California’s high-speed rail project has never lacked for drama, but the challenges converging on the California High-Speed Rail Authority in early 2026 represent something qualitatively different from the delays, cost overruns, and political skirmishes that have defined the project for the better part of two decades. The Authority now faces a reckoning that is simultaneously...

The post California High-Speed Rail at a Crossroads first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Lawmakers split on how to keep WisEye broadcasting
item.description:
Until last week, it appeared that legislators from both political parties were on a path to restoring WisconsinEye, an independent channel that has broadcast state government meetings and events since 2007.

The post Lawmakers split on how to keep WisEye broadcasting appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Early Literacy Reform Advances in the House
item.description:
Listen to this article Momentum for early literacy reform continues in Jefferson City, as House Bill (HB) 2872 recently passed out of committee. While this version removed several provisions from […]

The post Early Literacy Reform Advances in the House appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Arizona Supreme Court Weighs Jury Rights in the Administrative State
item.description:
The Arizona Supreme Court this morning heard arguments in a crucial case about whether bureaucratic agencies can disregard the constitutional..

The post Arizona Supreme Court Weighs Jury Rights in the Administrative State appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] School levy tax credits reward big spenders at the expense of frugal districts
item.description:
You might be paying higher school property taxes this year because of a referendum to exceed a school district’s revenue cap — one that you did not get a vote on in a district your kids do not attend.

The post School levy tax credits reward big spenders at the expense of frugal districts appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Virginiainstitute] The Pioneering Spirit of Microschools
item.description:
The Pioneering Spirit of Microschools: Insights from the Latest National Education Survey In an era where traditional institutions are increasingly questioned, the release of the 50CAN Educational Opportunity Survey’s second…

The post The Pioneering Spirit of Microschools first appeared on Virginia Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Crucial Badger-supported housing bill passes through Senate
item.description:
A bill that will ultimately help increase housing supply, make homes more affordable and still allow local municipalities to control where and how fast they grow passed through the Wisconsin Senate this week and was expected to pass in the Assembly Thursday as well.

The post Crucial Badger-supported housing bill passes through Senate appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Reality Check: NFL Economics BACK WHEN INDIANAPOLIS was selling its grandchildren to bring the Colts to town, an economist friend estimated that the contribution to the local economy would be equivalent to only three Walmarts. He was way off base. When spread over 365 days, the daily economic “foot traffic” impact of an NFL stadium falls between Read the full article...

item.title: [Virginiainstitute] Are Virginia’s Newest “Tax Cuts” Actually Tax Cuts?
item.description:
Campaign promises are easy to make, but often far harder to legislate. Last November, Virginia Democrats ran on what they called the “Affordable Virginia Plan,” promising to reduce the cost…

The post Are Virginia’s Newest “Tax Cuts” Actually Tax Cuts? first appeared on Virginia Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] As Other States Move Forward, Will New Jersey Leave Education Dollars Behind?
item.description:
By Wells Winegar, Executive Director, New Jersey Policy Institute   As Governor Mikie Sherrill begins her term in office, […]

item.description:
AdministrativeReformToolbox

The post The Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Cartel and Chinese Drones Demand Immediate FAA Action
item.description:
The Border Patrol and the military need clear authority to deploy counter-drone systems in sensitive areas without endless FAA vetoes. Drones are increasingly violating American airspace. We know that tens of thousands of drone sightings on our southern border are connected with the Mexican drug and human trafficking cartels. But dozens of other drone sightings at sensitive...

The post Cartel and Chinese Drones Demand Immediate FAA Action first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 7
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 7 It was a budget-heavy week under the Dome, with more than 100 appropriations bills clearing a critical hurdle. We unpack the latest on education funding, the lingering school choice conversation, and what to watch as the session heads toward its next deadline.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 7 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Mackinac] Why Michigan needs more housing, despite a flat population
item.description: What matters is the number of people who want to buy

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Governor Little makes a big commitment to expand educational opportunities for families
item.description: By opting into the federal scholarship tax credit program, Idaho continues to reinforce its commitment to education freedom and parental choice, ensuring families have greater access to resources that help tailor education to each child’s unique needs.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Trump Repeals EPA’s Endangerment Finding, Preserving Affordable Energy and Housing
item.description:
On February 12, the Trump Administration formally repealed the Environmental Protection Agency’s 2009 “endangerment finding” that gave the EPA authority to regulate greenhouse gas emissions from virtually anything that used energy, including vehicles, power plants, factories, dairy farms, landfills, fertilizer, rice paddies, tractors, air conditioners, refrigerators, etc. Reactions were swift. From CBS News, “an enormous blow to...

The post Trump Repeals EPA’s Endangerment Finding, Preserving Affordable Energy and Housing first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut’s Fiscal Guardrails Saved the State. Why Weaken Them Now?
item.description:
Connecticut lawmakers are once again eyeing the state’s fiscal guardrails — the bipartisan reforms widely credited with stabilizing the state’s finances less than a decade ago.  But many of the […]

The post Connecticut’s Fiscal Guardrails Saved the State. Why Weaken Them Now?  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Week in Review: Underdogs No More
item.description:
Going to court is hard enough without having the deck stacked against you from the start. In a major victory..

The post Week in Review: Underdogs No More appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Hochul continues to bribe Big Health with reckless Medicaid spending
item.description:
For the fourth year in a row, Gov. Kathy Hochul has used the word “unsustainable” to describe her own Medicaid budget. Give her....

The post Hochul continues to bribe Big Health with reckless Medicaid spending appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Winners & Losers: The Fight for Western Civilization (and beating Canada)
item.description:
Every Friday morning, I join the Cardle & Woolley Show on Talk 1370 Radio in Austin to announce the week’s Winners & Losers. Next week you can join us at the Texas Public Policy Foundation for Winners and Losers LIVE. Click here for more info.   See you there. Here’s who made this week’s list....

The post Winners & Losers: The Fight for Western Civilization (and beating Canada) first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Interview with Louisiana Works Secretary Susie Schowen
item.description:
Governor Landry recently issued an executive order “Modernizing Louisiana’s Workforce For A New Era.” Louisiana Works Secretary Susie Schowen breaks down what this means for job seekers and Louisiana’s growing economy.

The post Interview with Louisiana Works Secretary Susie Schowen appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] Supreme Court Restores the Rule of Law and, for a Moment, Increased Economic Freedom
item.description:
The Supreme Court’s decision in Learning Resources v. Trump is a landmark reaffirmation of one of the Constitution’s most fundamental principles: the power to tax belongs to Congress alone. Article I, Section 8 of the Constitution explicitly vests in the legislative branch the authority “to lay and collect Taxes, Duties, Imposts and Excises.” Tariffs are taxes, and the Court […]

The post Supreme Court Restores the Rule of Law and, for a Moment, Increased Economic Freedom appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Remembering the Legacy of George Washington
item.description:
George Washington was the indispensable leader of the American founding, held the new Republic together—from commanding the Continental Army, to presiding over the Constitutional Convention, to serving as the nation’s first President and peacefully relinquishing power. His moral authority was essential to the Constitution’s adoption and early stability, as he quelled internal unrest, navigated fierce factional disputes, upheld the rule of law, supported sound public finance, and set enduring precedents such as the two-term presidency. Washington’s enduring legacy rests on character and principle, including his commitment to constitutional government, national unity, religion and morality, and education—foundations that strengthened the Union and continue to serve as a model for ordered liberty.

The post Remembering the Legacy of George Washington appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] Mackinac Center partners for historic summit
item.description: 4 former Michigan governors make the undramatic case for civility

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke Book Review: A History of Money
item.description: by Mark Frank We think of money these days as anything but tangible. Venmo accounts, credit and debit cards, ACH transfers—we don’t really touch “real” money anymore. And so we can’t appreciate its importance in the development of civilization and our comfortable standard of living. “The History of Money: A Story of Humanity” (Henry Holt Read the full article...

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model
item.description:
IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) released its “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” on Monday, a practical handbook designed to help other states replicate Wisconsin’s reform framework. WHY IT MATTERS: In the 2010s, Wisconsin led one of the […]

The post IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Idaho Supreme Court victory for educational choice bodes well for Wyoming scholarship supporters
item.description: As one Idaho justice noted during oral arguments, lawmakers are capable of walking and chewing gum at the same time when it comes to education policy.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Your Electric Bill Is on the Ballot in 2026 — Whether You Know It or Not
item.description:
Here’s a pop quiz for the politically engaged: Who decides how much you pay for electricity? Your congressman? The governor? The White House? None of the above. It’s the five elected members of the Louisiana Public Service Commission (PSC). And in 2026, Louisiana voters will choose two new ones. Most people couldn’t pick a PSC...

The post Your Electric Bill Is on the Ballot in 2026 — Whether You Know It or Not appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Checking the Administrative State and Restoring Separation of Powers
item.description:
Stronger Legislative Oversight: Major regulations would require public transparency, detailed economic analysis, and approval by the Legislature before taking effect — ensuring elected lawmakers have final authority over costly rules. Independent Judicial Review: Courts would apply de novo review to major rules, meaning judges interpret the law independently rather than deferring to agency interpretations. Separate legislation would extend this standard further. Restoring Constitutional Balance: The proposal increases transparency, accountability, and oversight to rebalance power among the legislative, executive, and judicial branches.

The post Checking the Administrative State and Restoring Separation of Powers appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Time to tackle Hawaii’s healthcare crisis
item.description:
People around the world view Hawaii as a paradise. And in a lot of ways, it is. But for many of us who live here, our islands have become a place where we simply cannot afford to get sick.  The statewide physician shortage continues to worsen and is now estimated to be 833 full-time equivalent doctors. Our […]

The post Time to tackle Hawaii’s healthcare crisis appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Introducing Mississippi Momentum: A Blueprint for Lasting Prosperity
item.description:
Too many young people still leave Mississippi to chase opportunities elsewhere.  MCPP is on a mission to help change that - by creating the conditions for real, sustained growth so our children and grandchildren choose to stay, build lives, and thrive right here in our state. The good news?  Mississippi is no longer a laggard, but leading. item.title: [Mainepolicy] Ending the Sales Tax on Electricity
item.description:
Maine lawmakers are currently considering a proposal that would exempt all residential electricity from the state sales tax. While it may be a relatively small cut among all of Maine’s over-taxation and regulation, it is nevertheless good policy as well as an important acknowledgment of the growing reality that electricity costs are putting real pressure […]

The post Ending the Sales Tax on Electricity appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] The truth about Mexico’s cartel wars
item.description:
To understand the latest disturbing spasm of violence in Mexico, it helps to go back six years to an ultra-wealthy colonia called Lomas de Chapultepec, near the heart of Mexico City. Lomas de Chapultepec is protected, partly by a large security apparatus net that has been thrown around it, and partly by the pacto de narco, which protects...

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Why are so many governments serving as the collection agency for unions?
item.description: Government payroll systems are funded by taxpayers to administer public education — not to facilitate political fundraising.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Hospitals Turn a Safety-Net Program Into an ATM
item.description:
Washington, D.C. is finally taking a hard look at one of the most abuse-ridden federal health programs — the 340B Drug Pricing Program. Last week, the Trump administration released a request for information exploring ways to bring more transparency and accountability to the program. It’s about time.

The post Hospitals Turn a Safety-Net Program Into an ATM appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] House and Senate Budgets Propose No Major Tax or Spending Increases
item.description:
The Senate and House of Delegates’ financial committees met on Sunday to approve competing sets of amendments to the next Virginia budget, neither proposing any general tax increases. The Senate version included modest tax reform: a small taxpayer rebate for this year and an increase in the income tax standard deduction. The Senate, however, allows […]

The post House and Senate Budgets Propose No Major Tax or Spending Increases appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Johnlocke] CIA dumps documents spoiled by leftist activism
item.description:
Mollie Hemingway and Jordan Boyd write for the Federalist about a significant development at America’s top spy agency. Central Intelligence Agency Director John Ratcliffe ordered the retraction of 19 intelligence products following an independent review that found the documents failed to meet regulatory standards of tradecraft and quality, the agency announced Friday. Redacted versions of...

item.title: [Jbartlett] Is state aid to public education growing or shrinking?
item.description:
Is the state contribution to public education funding growing or shrinking?  The complexity of New Hampshire’s school funding formula, and misconceptions about how that formula works, cloud the answer.  But there are a few easy ways to cut through the fog:  State education aid primarily funds students, with some additional aid going to schools. The […]

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Speaker Arch on the 2026 Legislative Session
item.description:
On this episode of Nebraskanomics we welcome Senator John Arch, Speaker of the Nebraska Legislature. During the episode Speaker Arch and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss what’s expected this session, how property tax reform can happen in a tight budget year, and lessons from his time as speaker. Listen on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.

The post Nebraskanomics: Speaker Arch on the 2026 Legislative Session appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Iowa’s No-Shutdown Rule Clears First Funnel
item.description:
Iowa currently lacks a formal budget-continuation policy, meaning that if lawmakers fail to pass a General Fund budget by July 1, funding for many programs could technically lapse — creating uncertainty and potential separation-of-powers concerns. Senate File 2388, a proposed “no-shutdown rule,” would allow existing appropriations to automatically continue (excluding one-time spending) if a new budget is not enacted on time. The bill has already cleared Senate committee and passed the first legislative funnel. The goal is to promote stability and protect taxpayers, preventing political brinkmanship and service disruptions while aligning Iowa with sixteen other states that already have continuation laws in place.

The post Iowa’s No-Shutdown Rule Clears First Funnel appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.description:

item.title: [Empowerms] HB 1072 — Voluntary Portable Benefit Plan Act
item.description:
House Bill 1072, authored by Representatives Lee Yancey and Rodney Hall, creates a voluntary “portable benefits” framework for independent contractors and freelancers in Mississippi. The bill allows businesses or hiring parties to contribute money into benefit accounts that are owned by the independent worker, not the company. Those funds can be used for things like health…

The post HB 1072 — Voluntary Portable Benefit Plan Act first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] The court of repeated second chances strikes again
item.description:
Yesterday, Eddie Duncan, a 23-year-old man from North Minneapolis, bailed out of the Hennepin County jail where he was being held on a firearms charge. Within three hours Duncan shot and killed two of his cousins aged 13 and 23 in a relative’s home in North Minneapolis before being shot and killed during a shootout...

The post The court of repeated second chances strikes again appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Stop the Tape: The DFL vs the TRUTH

Grace, Kathryn, and Bill tackle the DFL’s absurd fixation on ICE, plus the drama surrounding the anti-fraud Inspector General bill....

item.title: [Americanexperiment] The habeas appeal
item.description:
It appears that the U.S. Department of Justice will be appealing that baseless contempt of court citation imposed by federal district Judge Laura Provinzino (Biden appointee). I covered the incident here, which involves a habeas corpus case captioned. Rigoberto Soto-Jiminez v. Bondi, et. al (File No. 26-cv-957). The facts are pretty straightforward: Mr. Soto is...

The post The habeas appeal appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 752 — Restroom, changing room, opp sex (+1)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 752 would make it a crime to enter a restroom or changing room designated for the opposite sex in a government-owned building or a place of public accommodation, with certain narrow exceptions. Rating: +1 NOTE: House Bill 752 deals with single-sex restrooms and changing rooms. It is a replacement for House Bill 606 […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 753 — Assertive community treatment (-6)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 753 would expand Medicaid to create an Assertive Community Treatment (ACT) program and team. Rating: -6 Does it create, expand, or enlarge any agency, board, program, function, or activity of government? Conversely, does it eliminate or curtail the size or scope of government? Medicaid is one of the largest and most expensive […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 754 — Direct primary care, phys therapy (+1)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 754 would clarify that physical therapists may participate in direct primary care (DPC) agreements with patients. Rating: +1 Does it give government any new, additional, or expanded power to prohibit, restrict, or regulate activities in the free market? Conversely, does it eliminate or reduce government intervention in the market? Subsection 6 of […]
